THE MATTER IS CONTINUED DUE TO A FEDERAL STAY IN BANKRUPTCY COURT. COUNSEL IS TO ADVISE THE COURT OF ANY MOVEMENT AS TO CERTAINTEED CORPORATION. DISCUSSIONS ARE HELD AS TO 524G AND THE TEXAS TWO-STEP CONTROVERSY. MS. LETSCH STATES THAT THEY ARE FIGHTING FOR A RETURN OF THESE MATTERS TO THE TORT SYSTEM, SO THESE CASES ARE LEFT ON THE COURT'S CALENDAR. MS. CHU STATES IF THEY DISMISS THAT THERE ARE CONCERNS AS TO WHETHER THEY WILL BE ABLE TO COME BACK AND THAT THERE WILL BE A GREAT PREJUDICE TO THEIR CLIENTS. THE COURT STATES THAT GOING FORWARD IT WOULD LIKE PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT ONE LETTER TO THE COURT AT LEAST EVERY SIX MONTHS. THE LETTER IS TO STATE IF A PLAN HAS BEEN PROPOSED, IS TO PROVIDE WHAT THE TIMING OF THE PLAN WILL BE, AND IS TO INCLUDE AN UPDATE ON THE CURRENT STATUS OF THE BANKRUPTCY. KIMBERLY CHU, ESQ., AND BRYN LETSCH, ESQ., OF BRAYTON PURCELL APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA ZOOM. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: J. ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. (304)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff and moving party William Angel.
Evanthia M. Spanos of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.
Neusha Etemad of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Water Application Distribution Group Inc.
Steven Spaulding of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant Ex-FM Inc.
Rochelle R. Ileto of DEMLER, ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP (415-949-1900), appearing for defendant Familian Corp.
Robert Kraft of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-397-9006), appearing for defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Co.

RULING: Hearing held, argued, and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date of March 15, 2021 is vacated. The trial date is April 5, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. The tolling of the trial date is May 3, 2021 per the December 10, 2020 Asbestos Case Management Department Order. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is April 16, 2021. The tolling date for the last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is May 17, 2021 per the December 10, 2020 Asbestos Case Management Department Order. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 29, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact and expert discovery remain closed. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 17, 2021. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. No later than January 28, 2021, plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than January 28, 2021, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendants' affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on March 17, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
